title: lachnopylis viscosa
intro: Lachnopylis viscosa, also known as the "sticky bush," is a type of plant that belongs to the family Rutaceae and is native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lachnopylis viscosa include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and even help to combat certain types of infections. Herbal preparations of Lachnopylis viscosa can be made into a tea, infusion, or cream, and are often used topically to apply the plant's medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
